back_translation: After these things had been done, the leaders approached me and said, “The people of Israel, with the priests and the Levites, have not separated themselves from the peoples of the lands, whose abominations are like those of the Canaanites, Hittites, Perizzites, Jebusites, Ammonites, Moabites, Egyptians, and Amorites.

For they have taken some of their daughters as wives for themselves and their sons, so that the holy seed has mixed with the peoples of the lands, and the hand of the leaders and officials has been foremost in this unfaithfulness.”
When I heard this, I tore my tunic and cloak, pulled hair from my head and beard, and sat down appalled.
Then everyone who trembled at the words of the God of Israel gathered around me because of the unfaithfulness of the exiles, while I sat appalled until the evening offering.
At the evening offering I rose from my humiliation, with my tunic and cloak still torn, and I knelt down and spread out my hands to the LORD my God,
and said: “O my God, I am ashamed and embarrassed to lift up my face to you, my God, for our iniquities have risen above our heads, and our guilt has grown up to the heavens.

From the days of our fathers to this day our guilt has been great, and because of our iniquities we, our kings, and our priests have been handed over to the kings of the lands, to the sword, to captivity, to plunder, and to humiliation, as it is this day.

But now, for a brief moment, grace has come from the LORD our God, to leave us a remnant and to give us a secure hold in his holy place, to brighten our eyes, O our God, and to give us a little reviving in our bondage.

For we are slaves, yet in our bondage our God has not forsaken us, but has extended to us steadfast love before the kings of Persia, giving us reviving to raise up the house of our God and repair its ruins, and giving us a wall of protection in Judah and Jerusalem.
And now, our God, what can we say after this? For we have forsaken your commandments,

which you commanded through your servants the prophets, saying: ‘The land that you are entering to possess is a land defiled by the impurity of the peoples of the lands, with their abominations that have filled it from one end to the other with their uncleanness.

Therefore do not give your daughters to their sons or take their daughters for your sons, and never seek their peace or prosperity, so that you may be strong and eat the good of the land and leave it as an inheritance to your children forever.’
After all that has come upon us because of our evil deeds and our great guilt—though you, our God, have punished us less than our iniquities deserve and have given us such a remnant as this—
shall we again break your commandments and intermarry with the peoples who commit these abominations? Would you not be angry with us until you destroyed us completely, leaving no remnant or survivor?
O LORD, God of Israel, you are righteous, for we are left this day as a remnant. Here we are before you in our guilt, though because of it no one can stand before you.”
